name: Rope Swing
description: A traversal state where the player is attached to a rope or pivot and moves along an arc under gravity and input.
design_notes: Rope swing is about controlled momentum. Players need to understand the pivot, rope length, release direction, and whether input pumps the swing. The mechanic becomes frustrating if collision, wrapping, or release timing behaves inconsistently.
design_purpose: Let players cross gaps through pendulum motion and timed release.
Create traversal challenges based on momentum, arc reading, and landing choice.
Support expressive movement that can combine with jump, grapple, or glide systems.
player_fantasy: The player feels like they are using momentum and timing to launch across space.

parameters.name: rope_length
parameters.description: Distance between pivot and player while attached.

parameters.name: swing_input_acceleration
parameters.description: Tangential acceleration added from player input.

parameters.name: swing_damping_ratio
parameters.description: Energy lost per second while swinging.

parameters.name: release_impulse_scale
parameters.description: Multiplier applied to current tangential velocity on release.

parameters.name: reattach_lockout_ms
parameters.description: Delay before the same rope can be grabbed again after release.

edge_cases: Rope collision can wrap around corners, clip through geometry, or force detachment; choose one behavior.
Release during pause, hit stop, or slow motion should use one authoritative velocity sample.
The player hitting a ceiling or wall mid-swing needs clear bounce, slide, or detach behavior.
Moving pivots must decide whether rope length and angular velocity are preserved.
common_bugs: Swing input adds world-horizontal force instead of tangential force and breaks the arc.
Rope length drifts over time because constraints are corrected after integration without projection.
Release velocity is sampled after collision correction and launches in the wrong direction.
Reattaching on the next frame traps the player in the same rope.
balancing_notes: Higher damping makes swings forgiving but less expressive; low damping rewards timing and route planning.
Large release impulse increases spectacle but can invalidate nearby platform spacing.
accessibility_notes: Provide clear release direction cues and consider wider landing areas when swing timing is mandatory.
Avoid camera swings that track the arc too aggressively; motion-sensitive players need stable horizon options.

implementation_notes.unity: Use a joint or custom pendulum constraint from FixedUpdate, add input as tangential acceleration, and sample release velocity before destroying the constraint.
implementation_notes.godot: Represent the rope as pivot position plus constrained radius, project the character back to rope_length each physics tick, and apply tangential input separately.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use a Physics Constraint or custom CharacterMovement mode for swing state, with the pivot replicated and release velocity applied authoritatively.
implementation_notes.web: Integrate angular velocity or constrained Cartesian motion in the simulation loop, project to rope_length after collision, and store release velocity before detach.

example_games.title: Donkey Kong Country 2
example_games.note: Uses rope swinging as a recurring platforming traversal challenge.

example_games.title: Spelunky
example_games.note: Uses ropes as traversal tools for vertical spaces.
